class Solution:
    def asteroidCollision(self, asteroids: list[int]) -> list[int]:
        stack = []
        for asteroid in asteroids:
            # Handle collisions if the current asteroid moves left (negative)
            # and there are asteroids in the stack moving right (positive)
            while stack and asteroid < 0 and stack[-1] > 0:
                # Case 1: Incoming asteroid is larger, stack top is destroyed
                if abs(asteroid) > stack[-1]:
                    stack.pop()
                    continue  # Keep checking with the new top of the stack (or if stack is empty)
                # Case 2: Both asteroids are of equal size, both destroyed
                elif abs(asteroid) == stack[-1]:
                    stack.pop()
                    asteroid = 0  # Mark current asteroid as destroyed
                    break  # No need to process this asteroid further
                # Case 3: Stack top is larger, incoming asteroid is destroyed
                else:  # abs(asteroid) < stack[-1]
                    asteroid = 0  # Mark current asteroid as destroyed
                    break  # No need to process this asteroid further
            
            # If the asteroid was not destroyed (either no collision, or it survived collisions)
            if asteroid != 0:
                stack.append(asteroid)
                
        return stack
